expected_effect: Preserve most of screen_0004's raw edge and arb discipline while recovering some retail edge and mean-fee spend in confirmed calm windows.
expected_failure_signature: If fee spend and retail edge remain pinned near screen_0004, the calm-window gate is still too weak; if raw edge drops back toward screen_0003, the reclaim leaked outside true non-toxic windows.
expected_win_condition: Stay near screen_0004 on mean_edge while reducing time_weighted_mean_fee and pulling retail_edge back above 540.
hidden_coupling_removed: screen_0004 still forces recovery cuts to stay uniformly tight even in confirmed calm windows, so non-toxic reclaim cannot reappear without reopening the full surface.
rationale: screen_0005 showed the previous non-toxic recovery gate was too strict. Keep screen_0004's stronger toxic overlay intact and restore only the healing-rebate and baseline-flex reductions from screen_0003->screen_0004 as calm-window increments on the non-toxic side.
title: Baseline split with gated calm-window reclaim
why_not_coefficient_retune: This preserves the screen_0004 split but changes when the recovery path reactivates by tying the recovered rebates to the existing calm-window gate, rather than loosening both sides globally.
